text: Research on ultramarathon running is predominantly observational, with 83.3% of publications being observational studies rather than experimental.
Race performance is the most studied topic in ultramarathon research, comprising 21.8% of all publications.

The rate of experimental studies in ultramarathon research has remained unchanged at a mean of 1.6 per year from 1999 to 2019, indicating a persistent gap in high-quality interventional evidence for ultramarathon training and racing practices.
Ultramarathon running research publication plateaued at approximately 49 annual publications from 2014 to 2018 after a rapid increase around 2010.
Ultramarathons can serve as a valuable model for studying physiological stress responses with relevance extending beyond ultramarathon runners to the wider population.
